stories.title: Add missing token columns to linkedin_org_integrations
acceptance: Migration adds access_token_encrypted, refresh_token_encrypted, token_expires_at columns to linkedin_org_integrations
Migration is idempotent (uses IF NOT EXISTS)
Dry-run passes on staging: npx supabase db push --linked --dry-run

stories.title: Fix OAuth state single-use and reduce TTL
acceptance: linkedin_oauth_states gets used_at column via migration
linkedin-oauth-callback sets used_at on first use, rejects if already used
State TTL reduced from 10 min to 5 min
Replayed OAuth callbacks return 400

stories.title: Add missing OAuth scopes for campaign write and conversions
acceptance: oauth-initiate/providers/linkedin.ts requests rw_ads and rw_conversions scopes
Scopes list is documented with which PRD requires each scope
OAuth flow correctly redirects with new scope set

stories.title: Commit untracked migrations and fix RLS INSERT policy
acceptance: 20260310090740_linkedin_integration_rls_write_policies.sql includes INSERT policy for linkedin_lead_sources
20260310091242_add_engagement_to_ad_library.sql committed
20260310092143_linkedin_matched_audiences.sql committed
20260310100238_add_is_saved_to_ad_library.sql committed
All 4 pass dry-run on staging

stories.title: Add webhook resolution failure logging table and rate limiting
acceptance: New linkedin_webhook_resolution_failures table created via migration
webhook-linkedin/index.ts logs failed resolutions to table instead of only console
In-memory rate limiting added: 10 req/min per IP (same pattern as demo-research)
Staging webhook rejects unsigned requests when LINKEDIN_CLIENT_SECRET is set

stories.title: Validate foundation fixes on staging
acceptance: npx supabase db push --linked --dry-run passes on staging
All LinkedIn edge functions deploy successfully to staging
OAuth connect flow works end-to-end with new scopes
Webhook receives test payload and processes without error

stories.title: Create LinkedInHub page shell with tab navigation
acceptance: New LinkedInHub.tsx page at /linkedin with ProspectingHub-style pill tabs
LinkedInHubTabs.tsx component with tabs: Overview, Leads, Campaigns, Analytics, Revenue, Ad Library, Audiences
URL param support: /linkedin?tab=campaigns deep-links to correct tab
routeConfig.ts updated: single /linkedin entry replaces 4 scattered entries
Old routes (/campaigns, /intelligence/ads, etc.) redirect to /linkedin?tab=<correct>

stories.title: Build LinkedIn Health Monitor header component
acceptance: LinkedInHealthMonitor.tsx component renders in hub header below tabs
Shows token expiry countdown (days remaining, red if <7 days)
Shows last sync timestamp with relative time
Shows webhook delivery rate (success % last 24h from linkedin_sync_runs)
Shows connection status badge (connected/disconnected/error)

stories.title: Build LinkedIn Onboarding Wizard
acceptance: LinkedInOnboardingWizard.tsx renders when !isConnected on hub page
Step 1: Connect LinkedIn (triggers OAuth flow)
Step 2: Select ad accounts from discovered accounts
Step 3: Enable lead gen form sync (toggles from LinkedInConfigModal)
Step 4: Quick actions (create campaign / explore ad library / done)
Wizard is dismissible and remembers dismissal in localStorage

stories.title: Build LinkedIn Overview Dashboard tab
acceptance: LinkedInOverviewTab.tsx renders as default tab in hub
Connection health card (status, token expiry, scopes)
Leads this week card (count, top ICP scores, trend arrow)
Campaign spend card (active count, total spend, top performer name + CTR)
Recent competitor ads card (last 5 from watchlist with thumbnails)
Quick action buttons: New Campaign, Search Ads, View Pipeline

stories.title: Refactor existing LinkedIn pages into hub tab components
acceptance: LinkedInLeadsTab.tsx wraps lead source config + recent leads from useLinkedInIntegration
LinkedInCampaignsTab.tsx wraps CampaignsPage content (removes Helmet, page-level chrome)
LinkedInAnalyticsTab.tsx wraps LinkedInAnalytics content
LinkedInRevenueTab.tsx wraps LinkedInRevenue content
LinkedInAdLibraryTab.tsx wraps AdLibrary content
Each tab loads data only when active (lazy load pattern)

stories.title: Build Audiences tab with Ops table push integration
acceptance: LinkedInAudiencesTab.tsx shows audience list with status badges (PENDING/PROCESSING/READY/FAILED)
Create audience dialog with name, type (CONTACT_LIST/COMPANY_LIST)
Ops table picker: select table, map email/company/domain columns
Push button triggers push_ops_to_audience action with selected rows
Status polling shows upload progress until READY or FAILED

stories.title: Campaign creation wizard with multi-step form
acceptance: CampaignWizard.tsx with steps: Objective, Format, Targeting, Budget/Bid, Schedule, Review
Objective picker: lead gen, website visits, conversions, engagement, brand awareness, video views
Format selector: single image, carousel, video, text ad, dynamic ad
Targeting builder: job title, function, seniority, company, industry, geography
Budget input: daily/lifetime with currency, bidding strategy (manual CPC/CPM, target cost)
Review step shows full summary, creates campaign in DRAFT status

stories.title: Creative builder with AI copy generation
acceptance: CreativeBuilder.tsx allows upload/select images and videos
AI copy generation button generates headline, body, CTA from campaign objective + ICP data
Preview in desktop feed, mobile feed layouts
Validation enforces format constraints (creative format must match campaign type)
Active creative count warning at 15 per campaign

stories.title: Lead gen form builder and campaign linking
acceptance: LeadFormBuilder.tsx with field selection (name, email, company, title, phone, custom)
Form preview showing LinkedIn lead form appearance
Form linked to campaign during creative setup
List, edit, archive existing lead gen forms
Form responses flow through Lead Response Copilot pipeline (existing)

stories.title: Anomaly detection engine for advertising analytics
acceptance: linkedin-analytics-sync detects anomalies: sudden cost spikes (>50% day-over-day), CTR drops (>30%), lead volume declines (>40%)
Anomaly detection runs after each sync cycle
Detected anomalies stored in linkedin_campaign_alerts table
Each alert includes severity, suggested action (pause, adjust targeting, review creative)
Slack notification for high-severity anomalies via shared deliverySlack infrastructure

stories.title: Demographics pivot UI and CSV export for analytics
acceptance: Demographic pivot selector: job title, function, seniority, industry, company size, geography
Bar chart and table view for each pivot showing impressions, clicks, spend, leads
Top-performing segments highlighted with badge
CSV export button generates download for any analytics view
Date range filtering with presets (7d, 30d, 90d, custom)

stories.title: Revenue campaign quality dashboard and Slack alerts
acceptance: Campaign quality view in Revenue tab: lead volume vs meetings vs proposals per campaign
Campaigns with high leads but poor downstream conversion are flagged with warning badge
Slack alert fired when campaign quality score drops below threshold
Alert includes recommendation: pause review, audience review, creative review
Cost per meeting, cost per deal, ROAS displayed per campaign

stories.title: Ad Library battlecard/proposal integration and weekly digest
acceptance: Ad Library trend view shows weekly angle frequency changes
Competitor ad data accessible from battlecard skill via copilot context
Weekly Slack digest summarizes new ads from tracked competitors
Digest configurable: frequency, competitors, detail level
Likely winner scoring surfaces ads with longevity + variant density signals

stories.title: Bidirectional campaign sync drift detection UI
acceptance: Campaign sync detects when a campaign was modified in LinkedIn Campaign Manager
Drift badge appears on affected campaigns in the list view
Drift detail dialog shows what changed (targeting, budget, status, creative)
Accept remote changes button updates local state to match LinkedIn
Bulk accept for multiple drifted campaigns

stories.title: Cross-feature attribution chain schema and service
acceptance: New linkedin_attribution_events table: id, org_id, event_type, source_entity_id, source_entity_type, target_entity_id, target_entity_type, metadata JSONB, created_at
Event types: ad_library_insight, campaign_created, lead_captured, contact_created, deal_created, deal_won
RLS policies scoped to org membership
Service function to record attribution events from edge functions

stories.title: Wire attribution into Overview Dashboard and pipeline overlay
acceptance: Overview Dashboard shows attribution chain summary card
Card displays: Ad Library insights → campaigns created → leads captured → deals → revenue
Click-through from any node to the source entity
Pipeline overlay on Analytics tab links campaigns to downstream pipeline outcomes

stories.title: Ops table lead tracking and campaign performance views
acceptance: LinkedIn-sourced leads appear in standard Leads Ops table with source=linkedin column populated
Campaign performance available as read-only standard Ops table (campaign name, spend, leads, meetings, deals)
Audience push from Ops table shows status in audience management UI
Source attribution visible on contact records (which campaign/form generated the lead)

stories.title: Event-to-Pipeline schema and edge functions
acceptance: Migration creates: linkedin_event_connections, linkedin_events, linkedin_event_registrants, linkedin_event_sync_runs tables
linkedin-event-sync edge function connects to Events Management API and pulls event + registrant data
Registrants are deduplicated by LinkedIn member ID per event
Sync is idempotent and supports both one-time import and ongoing updates
RLS policies scoped to org membership

stories.title: Event registrant prioritization and matching
acceptance: Registrants ranked by ICP fit, account value, existing pipeline, and owner relevance
Existing contacts/companies matched before new records created (reuse Lead Copilot matching)
High-priority registrants flagged with priority tier (hot/warm/cold)
Existing customers and open deals flagged clearly on registrant records

stories.title: Pre-event coordination and post-event follow-up engine
acceptance: Pre-event Slack summary with top registrants, target accounts, open deals
Post-event segmentation: attended, engaged, no-show, unknown
Attendee follow-up drafts reference event topic and next logical step
No-show follow-up emphasizes recap and next engagement opportunity
Follow-up drafts respect HITL approval (same as Lead Copilot)

stories.title: Events tab UI with registrant list and reporting
acceptance: LinkedInEventsTab.tsx added to hub with event list, registrant table, follow-up status
Event detail view: registrant count, priority breakdown, follow-up completion rate
Event-to-pipeline report: registrations → meetings booked → deals → revenue
Event registrant list available as auto-provisioned Ops table
Connect event button triggers LinkedIn Events API sync

stories.title: Graph Import schema and archive parser
acceptance: Migration creates user-scoped: linkedin_archive_imports, linkedin_import_contacts, linkedin_import_messages, linkedin_import_relationship_scores
Parser handles LinkedIn Connections.csv: first name, last name, email, company, position, connected_on
Parser handles message export files: sender, recipient, date, subject, content
Import is idempotent per user per import run
RLS policies scoped to importing user (not org-wide)

stories.title: Trust scoring engine and CRM contact matching
acceptance: Trust scoring: connection + no messages = weak, inbound-only = low trust, bidirectional = trusted, frequent + recent = strong
Trust tiers computed: cold, known, trusted, strong
CRM matching: LinkedIn URL match > name + company heuristics
Uncertain matches flagged for manual review
Scores stored in linkedin_import_relationship_scores

stories.title: Network tab UI with archive upload wizard and trust overlay
acceptance: LinkedInNetworkTab.tsx added to hub tabs
Archive upload wizard (reuses CSVImportOpsTableWizard pattern): upload → parse → review → import
Import summary: total contacts, matched CRM contacts, unmatched, trust tier breakdown
Trust tier badges on matched contacts in contact detail views
Clear labeling: 'Your LinkedIn relationship signal' (user-scoped, not org-wide)

parallelGroups.note: Events and Graph Import can start simultaneously
